evals.prompt: Run /hotfix the login endpoint is returning 500 errors in production, users can't sign in
evals.expected_output: Creates critical bug issue, branches from main, diagnoses root cause, implements minimal fix with tests, merges to both main AND develop, creates hotfix tag
expectations: Creates a GitHub Issue with label type:hotfix and priority:critical
Uses systematic debugging to find root cause before fixing
Implements a minimal fix (smallest change possible)
Writes at least one test for the fix
Creates PR via gh pr create --base main and merges hotfix branch to main
Creates PR via gh pr create --base develop --head main (backmerge to develop, prevents regression)
Creates a git tag with hotfix version
Restores previous .state.md workflow if one was in progress
Commit message does NOT include Co-Authored-By or AI attribution

evals.prompt: Run /hotfix while a feature workflow is in progress on branch feature/add-dashboard
evals.expected_output: Warns about existing workflow but proceeds (hotfix takes priority), then restores previous state after completion
expectations: Reads .state.md and notices an existing workflow
Warns the user about the in-progress work
Proceeds with the hotfix despite existing workflow (hotfix takes priority)
After hotfix is complete, restores the previous workflow state
